summary: Added generated-art Chapter Game Portal to the selected Path Map chapter dossier so each chapter visibly bridges trail evidence into the lane-specific minigame module.

queries: game hub portal UI level select minigame launcher design
dashboard game launcher card UX game portal design
gamification UX progress minigame dashboard design
pattern_notes: Gamified surfaces should clarify progress and action, not distract from the work record.
Launcher and hub references are useful when they show a clear bridge from current mission state into a playable module.
The portal must be data-derived from lane minigame fields so future standalone repos inherit it without hardcoded lane names.

generated_asset.prompt_summary: Premium dark sci-fi chapter-to-minigame portal launch bay texture with a luminous portal ring, stage pads, teal/amber/green glows, no text, no logos, and no characters.

ui_contract: Render a path-chapter-game-portal panel inside the selected chapter record deck.
Use generated path-chapter-game-portal-20260618.png as the universal chapter-to-game portal backdrop.
Bind the panel to lane.visual.minigame texture, title, mechanic, status, custom renderer state, game step count, and lane asset coverage.
Expose GAME, FORGE, and TRAIL actions through existing local detail-view controls.
Keep animation transform/opacity-based and covered by reduced-motion handling.
Register the generated portal texture in the Visual Asset Vault system assets.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_game_portal.py -q failed before implementation on missing path-chapter-game-portal-20260618.png.
red_green.green: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_game_portal.py -q passed after generated asset, component, styles, docs, cache token, and Asset Vault wiring.

responsive.evidence: All checked viewports rendered the Chapter Game Portal with generated backdrop, six stats, three actions, zero bad rects, zero button text overflow, zero page horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.
